pattern:
(?:
       (["']).+?\1 # [0: quote mark] quoted string, e.g. "hi there"
       |           # or
       (?:^|\s)    # beginning of value or whitespace
       (?:
        \d+        # any number of digits, e.g. 123
        |          # or
        \d*\.?\d+  # any number of digits with decimal, e.g. 1.23 or .123
       )
       ([a-z%]+)   # [1: units] letters or percent sign, e.g. px or %
      )

attack_strings.prefix:
# or
       	    # beginning of value or whitespace
                 # or

pattern:
\s*
                     class=('|")(.+?)\1     ## note: use non-greedy match e.g. .+?

\s*
                     style=('|").+?\1     ## note: use non-greedy match e.g. .+?
]* src\s*=\s* (?: (?# Match quoted attribute) (['"])file://(?P[^>]+)\1 (?# Match unquoted attribute, which may not contain spaces) | file://(?P[^>\s]+) ) [^>]* >

# EXAMPLE: <a rel="nofollow" href="{mailto}webmaster"

        (?P<markup><\s*[^\>]*   # <a rel="nofollow" href=   --> markup
            (?:href|src|poster|data|cite|formaction|action)\s*=)

        (?P<quote>["\'])        # "                         --> quote
        \{(?P<cmd>.*?)\}        # {mailto}                  --> cmd
        (?P<url>.*?)            # webmaster                 --> __url (see path)
        \2                      # "                         <-- quote
]* src\s*=\s* (?: (?# Match quoted attribute) (['"])file://(?P[^"]+)\1 (?# Match unquoted attribute, which may not contain spaces) | file://(?P[^>\s]+) ) [^>]* >
